Empires & Puzzles Jasper Review: The Gentle Bull of Springvale
Jasper brings big buffs and healing to the Springvale party, but his slow mana speed is a major hurdle. Find out if his massive critical chance boost is enough to earn him a spot on your team.
Jasper Review: Is This Flower-Loving Bull Worth the Wait?
Jasper: The Flower-Loving Bull Who Takes His Sweet Time

Jasper arrived in the Springvale Festival of 2023, bringing a gentle demeanor and a basket full of sunflowers to the battlefield. As a 5-star Holy Cleric, he plays the role of a support booster, offering healing and significant offensive buffs to his allies. While his intentions are noble, his Slow mana speed and lack of immediate impact have made him a polarizing figure. This review focuses on the base version of Jasper, a hero who shines brightest when time is on his side—which, unfortunately, isn't often in today's fast-paced meta.

Jasper's artwork is a delightful break from the usual armored warriors and terrifying monsters. He's a large, anthropomorphic bull wearing a gardener's apron, affectionately holding a basket of sunflowers. The warm yellows and earthy tones perfectly capture the Springvale aesthetic. It is hard to be intimidated by a cow who looks like he just won first prize at the county fair for his floral arrangement, but perhaps that's part of his charm. He lures you in with cottage-core vibes before... well, healing you slowly.
Here is a look at Jasper's base stats.

When we look at Jasper's stats through the lens of the modern game, the picture is a bit wilted. With a base Power of 868 and stats hovering around 797 Attack and 869 Defense, he falls significantly behind the latest power creep curve where heroes regularly boast stats over 1300. He is reasonably bulky for his era, but compared to the juggernauts released recently, he feels quite fragile. His health pool is decent, but without the modern stat bloat, he relies heavily on emblems and limit breaks to survive long enough to fire his slow skill.
"Petals of Nourishment" is a skill that looks fantastic on paper but struggles in practice due to the delivery system.
1428 HP over 6 turns is a massive amount of health (238 per turn), but 6 turns is an eternity in Empires & Puzzles. Most battles are decided before the full heal can tick.
The +50% Attack and +50% Critical Chance are the real selling points. These are substantial boosts that can turn tile damage into nuclear strikes.

The caster gets -5% mana generation. For a Slow hero, this is just cruel. It makes recharging him even more painful, essentially ensuring he likely only fires once per battle unless you are playing Rush Attack.
Slow. With the -5% mana generation ailment he casts on himself, his second charge is effectively "Very Slow."
238 HP per turn. It is a strong Heal over Time, but lacks the immediate rescue potential of burst healers.
+50% Attack and Crit essentially doubles your tile damage output on average. Against Titans, this is where the math works in his favor.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Jasper's kit is relatively simple, relying mostly on his family bonus as he lacks modern passives.
The Springvale family bonus provides an Attack boost (10%/20% for 2/3 heroes). This synergizes well with his role as an offensive buffer, pushing tile damage even higher.
Jasper does not possess any modern passive skills, which further highlights his age compared to newer heroes who often come with two or three innate abilities.

This is a practical, if boring, choice. Since Jasper needs to survive to fire his slow skill, extra defense is always welcome. However, it doesn't offer the game-changing potential of something like Ailment Immunity or Revive.
Upon release, Jasper was seen as a "Titan specialist" and a niche pick for Rush Attack wars. He never really dominated the meta like some of his Springvale cousins (looking at you, Killhare).

The game has moved towards speed and immediate impact. Slow heroes who provide effects "over time" are at a severe disadvantage. Heroes like Hathor or even older healers provide overheal or immediate immunity at faster speeds.

Today, Base Jasper is largely a benchwarmer for competitive play. His stats are too low, and his speed is too slow. His only remaining stronghold is on Titan teams for players who lack faster critical buffers, or perhaps in very specific Rush Tournament scenarios.
Comparing Base Jasper to his Costume is like comparing a bicycle to a motorcycle.
The Costume (reviewed separately) changes him to the Monk class and significantly boosts his numbers.
The Costume increases the buffs to +60% Attack and +60% Critical Chance, and condenses the healing into 5 turns (making the per-turn heal stronger).
The Costume Bonus provides a massive stat injection that helps patch up his outdated base stats.
If you have the Costume, there is almost no reason to ever use the Base version. The Costume is superior in every single metric.
If you are using Base Jasper, survival is your only priority.
Maximizes his chances of surviving until he can cast his special. His high base HP benefits nicely from percentage-based nodes.
His tile damage remains mediocre, though his buff helps compensate.
path is recommended because a dead Jasper provides zero buffs. He needs to be a meat shield that eventually makes your team hit harder.
+50% Attack and Crit is a potent combination for tile damage.
If the battle drags on, the total HP recovered is immense.
As a Cleric, he has a chance to resist mana control, which helps slightly with his speed issue.
Jasper is a buffer first and a healer second. His ability to amplify damage is his saving grace.
Slow mana is a death sentence in modern raids, and the self-inflicted mana slow makes it worse.
He cannot save a teammate from the brink of death immediately.
All his buffs can be wiped away by a fast dispeller, leaving you with nothing after waiting for him to charge.
The -5% mana generation on himself is an unnecessary handicap for a hero who is already slow.
He is too slow, too vulnerable to dispel, and arguably hurts himself more than he helps in fast-paced matches.
His best role. Pop a mana potion, fire him, and watch your tile damage soar against Dark Titans.
In 5-star Rush Tournaments or Wars, his speed becomes Very Fast, making his buffs terrifyingly oppressive.
In long PvE battles where speed matters less, his sustain can be useful.
Jasper is not a hero you build a defense team around. He is a tool for specific offensive tasks.
- Joon - Ranvir/Wu Kong. Use Jasper for the Crit and Attack buff to maximize tile damage during stuns.
- Alfrike - Khufu - Kalø. In Rush, he ensures your heavy hitters strike with lethal force while keeping them healthy.
Sabina, Melendor, or any hero who removes buffs makes Jasper useless.
Heroes like Mist or Hipnos prevent him from applying his buffs entirely.
Proteus or Hel will ensure he never charges in the first place.

If Jasper is your first 5-star healer, treat him with patience. He will serve you well in map stages and against Titans. However, do not prioritize him over faster, more versatile healers like Rigard or Kiril for general raiding.

Base Jasper is effectively a collector's item or a specific tool for Mythic Titans if you are chasing high scores and lack better Crit buffers. Do not spend Alpha Aethers on the base version. If you don't have the costume, he likely belongs on your bench or in the Soul Exchange.
The community reaction to Jasper was lukewarm at best.
One player noted, "This hero would be quite good if he had an overheal over time... beside that, he is quite underwhelming."
Another accurately predicted his fate: "To me screams Titan hero. Not very interested in him."
The general sentiment was that while the buffs were nice, the slow speed and lack of immediate impact made him "fragile" and "not for defense for sure."

Jasper is a hero who means well. He wants to nurture your team with flowers and health, but he just takes too long to get the job done. In a game dominated by speed and violence, a slow gardener is out of place. While he finds a comfortable niche fighting Titans, for most other content, he is simply too slow to bloom.
A niche Titan support hero who struggles to find his footing elsewhere. Use him for the critical hits, but don't expect him to save the day in a raid.

A detailed review of Jasper, the 5-star Holy hero from the Springvale family. We analyze his stats, slow mana speed, and whether his critical chance buff makes him worth your darts in the current meta.
